question_reference.text: What supernatural substance fuels a vampire's powers and keeps them animated?
What do vampires call the primal hunger and rage that threatens to overwhelm their humanity?
What's the name for the death-like sleep that vampires enter for years or centuries?
What trait determines how potent a vampire's blood is and how much they can store?
Every vampire shows two faces: one a false persona they show to others and which maintains the Masquerade, the other their true nature and dark secret, hidden from the world. Name them.
What mortal connections keep a vampire grounded and prevent them from becoming a monster?
What are the three traditional weaknesses that define all vampires?
What drives a vampire to hunt and makes them monstrous?
What do you call a mortal who regularly drinks vampire blood and gains minor powers?
What happens when a vampire loses control and the Beast takes over completely?
What are the Three Traditions that govern vampire society?
Name the four main covenants that structure Kindred politics.
List the three age categories of vampires and their approximate year ranges.
What do vampires call their neutral gathering places where violence is forbidden?
What typically happens to vampires who refuse to join any covenant?
What's the term for a vampire's claim to rule over a city?
Name three common court positions or titles.
Why do vampires risk attending court despite the dangers?
What do you call a territory that a vampire controls and feeds from?
Can Disciplines be used in Elysium according to 2nd Edition rules?
Name the three physical Disciplines.
Identify the Discipline unique to each clan: Daeva: ? Gangrel: ? Mekhet: ? Nosferatu: ? Ventrue: ?
How many levels does each Discipline have?
What do you call custom powers created by combining multiple Disciplines?
How can a vampire learn a Discipline outside their clan?
What trait makes vampires more resistant to Disciplines than mortals?
Can you use Disciplines on other vampires in Elysium?
What resources are typically spent to activate Disciplines?
True or False: All Disciplines require a dice roll to activate.
Name two Disciplines that primarily affect the mind.
Name the four covenant-specific powers or blood sorceries.
Which covenant's power can suppress or interfere with other vampires' Disciplines?
Which covenant's blood sorcery involves blood sacrifice and primal rites?
Which covenant's power invokes biblical miracles and divine judgement?
Which covenant's power creates binding supernatural contracts between vampires?
Under what circumstances can a vampire learn one of the covenant blood sorceries?
What typically fuels covenant powers besides Vitae?
Which blood sorcery is described as channelling the power of the Beast itself?
Can a vampire switch covenants and keep their previous covenant's powers?
What makes covenant powers different from regular Disciplines?
What is Blood Sympathy and how does it work?
What's the difference between a bloodline and a clan?
Name the five clans and their stereotypical roles.
What do vampires call themselves in their own society?
Which clan's curse involves obsession with those they feed upon?

answers.q07: Sunlight, Fire, Wooden stake through the heart (Decapitation while staked is a true death)
answers.q10: 1. Frenzy: loss of control, no restraint, transformation into a mindless predator; 2. Loss of humanity - the price paid for the Beast taking over
answers.q11: 1. The Tradition of Masquerade Vampires must keep their existence secret from mortals. Revealing the truth of the Kindred to humanity risks destruction for everyone.  2. The Tradition of Progeny No vampire may create another vampire without permission from the domain’s ruler (usually the Prince). Unauthorized Embraces are considered serious crimes.  3. The Tradition of Amaranth A vampire may not commit diablerie—the act of consuming another vampire’s soul—except under the sanction of the domain’s ruler. Unauthorized Amaranth is one of the gravest offenses in Kindred society.
answers.q12: 1. The Invictus – An aristocratic covenant that values hierarchy, tradition, and the right of the powerful to rule.  2. The Lancea et Sanctum – A religious covenant that believes vampires are cursed instruments of divine will and must rule through faith and discipline.  3. The Carthian Movement – A political covenant that experiments with modern systems of governance such as democracy, councils, and revolutions.  4. The Circle of the Crone – A spiritual covenant that worships ancient, primal forces and views vampirism as a sacred transformation tied to cycles of nature and the Dark Mother.
answers.q13: 1. Neonate: Embraced within the last 50 years (after 1975) - Most new vampires, still learning Kindred society; 2. Ancilla: Embraced 50-150 years ago (1875-1975) - Established vampires with experience and connections (plural - ancillae); 3. Elder: Embraced more than 150 years ago (before 1875) - Ancient and powerful, but rare
answers.q15: Unaligned Vampires trade freedom for security. They exist outside of the political structures of Kindred society which can decrease their protection, allies, and influence. Also membership grants access to supernatural magical abilities. But being unaligned might mean being able to move more freely, might reduce the number of enemies, or and doesn't demand certain loyalties (so downtime actions can be completely self serving for instance)
answers.q17: Primogen (influential power figures), Socialites (reputation-makers), and Enforcers (order maintainers).
answers.q18: Vampires attend court because it is where power, information, and influence are exchanged. Despite the dangers, court provides opportunities to negotiate alliances, resolve disputes, gain favour, and stay informed about the political landscape of the domain. Avoiding court risks isolation, suspicion, and loss of influence within Kindred society.
answers.q22: Daeva: Majesty;  Gangrel: Protean;  Mekhet: Auspex;  Nosferatu: Nightmare;  Ventrue: Dominate
answers.q25: "To learn another clan’s unique Discipline, the vampire must first track down another vampire who possesses the Discipline and drink at least one point of his Vitae — with all of the attendant consequences."   1. Covenant Disciplines require membership to the covenant and a teacher;  2. Clan Uncommon Disciplines - clan based; requires drinking a vitae and a teacher with a dot in that discipline; 3. Bloodline - joining a bloodline unlocks specific disciplines
answers.q30: 1. Circle of the Crone - Cruac;  2. Carthian Movement - Carthian Law;  3. Lancea et Sanctum - Theban Sorcery;  4. Invictus - Blood Oaths
answers.q35: A vampire can learn a covenant blood sorcery by joining that covenant and being initiated or taught by someone who already knows the practice. Can also access a covenant blood sorcery by earning status in another covenant and gaining access to the benefits of the covenant if they have a willing teacher
answers.q36: 1. Crone - Cruac fuelled by blood and primal gods; paid in humanity it is a breaking point for Humanity 4 or higher and knowing the discipline caps humanity at 10 -Cruac dots; 2. Lancea - Thelan Sorcery fuelled by concentrated faith, mentally and time consuming which requires a physical sacrement; must have a Humanity rating equal to or higher than the dot rating of any miracle attempted; 3. Carthian Law Merits -  Requires a certain amount of Carthian Status and has drawback effects depending on the Law; 4. Invictus - Oaths requires a Notary to officiate the Oath; an Invictus must have at least one active Oath for their induction; the are two way streets; fuelled by contracts with drawbacks depending on the oath
answers.q38: Yes but it is limited. A vampire who leaves a covenant generally keeps the covenant powers they already learned, but they usually cannot learn new ones without membership in that covenant. Some powers may also become harder to practice without access to the covenant’s rituals, teachers, or support.
answers.q39: Covenant powers differ from regular Disciplines because they are ritualistic or philosophical abilities tied to a specific covenant’s teachings and traditions, rather than innate vampiric powers shared across clans. They usually require initiation, training, and special rites, and often involve more preparation or symbolic components than standard Disciplines.
answers.q40: Bloody Sympathy is a supernatural link between vampires who share the same bloodline, letting them feel each other’s strong emotions and major events like frenzy, torpor, or death. The closer the relationship (like sire and child), the stronger and clearer the connection, and the farther it can reach. These feelings are usually vague/symbolic. Close relatives can try to send each other signals using Willpower. It happens automatically during intense moments and is mainly used to create drama, sometimes even causing a vampire to frenzy.
answers.q41: A clan is one of the five major vampire families that all Kindred belong to (Daeva, Gangrel, Mekhet, Nosferatu, Ventrue.  A bloodline can be a branch of a clan that has changed to something altogether different which has developed unique traits, powers, or curses while still belonging to that parent clan. A clan is an umbrella; a blood line is a condition towards a specific, familial abnormality. A clan is inate; but a bloodline can be joined via an Avus (mentor). Clans are base lines and set; a bloodline can be created by vampires with blood potency 6. a Bloodline can have unique disciplines, exclusive devotions or strange sorceries that can not be learned by others outside the bloodline even through diablerie.
answers.q42: 1. Daeva – Seducers and tempters; passionate, charismatic vampires who manipulate through desire and emotion.;  2. Gangrel – Predators and survivors; feral hunters tied to instinct, nature, and physical resilience.;   3. Mekhet – Shadows and scholars; secretive investigators, spies, and keepers of occult knowledge.;   4. Nosferatu – Monsters and terrors; unsettling figures who rule through fear, intimidation, and information.;   5. Ventrue – Kings and rulers; aristocratic leaders who dominate through authority, discipline, and control.

answers.q15: They are “Unaligned” and they are left out in the cold to fend for themselves, eventually they go nuts
answers.q18: Cause they crave company of their own kind, the only people who truely understand their “lives”
answers.q20: Safety from direct physical harm. Sometimes, no feeding.
answers.q25: The uncommon disciplines (auspex, nightmare, dominate, majesty, protean) require blood from someone with that discipline. It need not be in clan for them, and it need not be the same vampire teaching it to you.
answers.q30: Invictus - Oaths, Carthians - Law, Lance - Theban, Crone - Cruac
answers.q35: Typically you need a dot in the related Covenant's status to learn the then associated sorcery.
answers.q39: They are the advantage you gain for joining a Covenant. Some are disciplines, some are other advantages. You have to be a member of a covenant to gain access to them and involve a level of commitment. Most other powers don’t have that requirement.
answers.q40: Blood Sympathy is a vampire’s familiar tie to their lineage. It can manifest in several ways, typically a mystical bond that gives your character a sense of how a relative feels, and can provide circumstantial bonuses on certain rolls.
answers.q41: A Clan is one of the Core Five kinds of Vampires. Bloodlines are offshoots from the Core Clans that have themes and often unique powers.
answers.q42: Daeva - Passion and Seduction, Gangrel - Staunch, Primal and Feral, Mekhet - Sneaky and Inquisitive, Nosferatu - Scary Spooky, Ventrue - Blue Blooded Lords.

answers.q08: The Beast, again? Also hunger, low humanity.
answers.q12: Carthian Movement, Invictus, Lancea et Sanctum, Circle of the Crone
answers.q13: Neonate (<50 years) Ancilla (50-150 years) Elder (150+ years)
answers.q15: Bad things. They may be slave to whomever wishes to use them, they might be destroyed out of hand, or simply forced into the least desireable hunting grounds. Whatever it is, it's bad.
answers.q18: The Danse Macabre. Without that purpose, what are they? Besides, as dangerous as it is to attend, it is more dangerous to be out of the loop.
answers.q19: Hunting ground. Sometimes domain, or demesne, or Manor, or hood
answers.q25: With a taste of vitae from someone with the discipline, and a teacher. They need not be the same individual, and it need not be in clan for either of them.
answers.q30: Carthian Lore, Invictus Oaths, Theban Sorcery, Cruac
answers.q31: All of them in different ways, but primarily Carthian Lore
answers.q39: They require teaching, with the exception of Carthian Lore which ios emergent from philosophy and covenant membership. They are not inherent in the blood.
answers.q40: Spidey sense for what is happening to close relatives. It gives glimpses of strong emotion, most reliably with the closest relatives.
answers.q41: There are five clans and many bloodlines. Clans have three disciplines, bloodlines have a bonus fourth on top of those of the parent clan. Bloodlines can be joined at differing degrees of remove, depending on the BP of the petitioning Kindred.
answers.q42: Ventrue: Kings - Gangrel:savages and wanderers - Nosferatu:haunts - Mekhet:shadows - Daeva:socialites

answers.q07: Sunlight, fire and a wooden stake through the heart
answers.q11: Sire progeny at your own peril, maintain the masquerade, do not diablerise your fellow kindred
answers.q12: The Invictus, The Carthian Movement, The Lancea et Sacntum, and the Circle of the Crone
answers.q18: Upholding The Masquerade means that Kindred must act as if they are mortal, whereas at Court a Kindred may let down the façade and communicate openly about their plans and schemes with other folk who share their condition - Court is a risk because it is where the most open discussion of Vampire activities occurs but it is one of the few places where the Kindred can freely speak about their condition without the presence of mortals
answers.q20: Yes, but only in a non-violent manner
answers.q25: Animalism,  Celerity, Obfuscate, Resilience, and Vigor may be learnt independently by any Kindred without a tutor / Auspex, Dominate, Majesty, Nightmare, and Protean require a tutor to be learnt and a drop of Vitae from the respective clan /  Theban Sorcery and Cruac require a teacher but do not require Vitae / disciplines unique to a bloodline can not be learnt ever, even through diablerie
answers.q30: Invictus Oaths, Carthian Law, Theban Sorcery, Cruac
answers.q35: They must have at least one dot in Covenant Status in the respective Covenant - LeS for Theban Sorcery and Circle for Cruac
answers.q36: Willpower, as well as rituals requiring multiple participants, time investments and sacrifice
answers.q39: Covenant powers require membership with a covenant and can not be taught to non-members, and are able to have scope beyond an individual vampire, including city-wide effects
answers.q40: Vampires of the same clan possess a mystical link between one another - this can alert you when a member of your Clan meets Final Death or enters a Torpor, increases the effectiveness of certain Disciplines, and provides a bonus to detect the presence of a relation - Blood Sympathy is stronger depending on how closely related the Vampires are (sire, grandsire, etc.)
answers.q41: A clan is a subset of a bloodline, a specific strain that has all the benefits and downsides of its parent clan as well as its own unique features
answers.q42: Daeva - seducers, charmers, celebrities / Nosferatu -  boogeymen, hermits, spies / Mekhet - archivists, investigators, occultists / Gangrel - bodyguards, hunters, warriors / Ventrue - businessfolk, nobles, politicians

answers.q07: Fire, sunlight, the need for blood
answers.q12: Invictus, Lancea et Sanctum, Carthian, Circle of the Crone
answers.q15: Bad things. Very bad things. (please note that I am _technically_ correct - the best kind of correct!)
answers.q18: To influence the politics and power struggles of their home city.
answers.q22: Daeve = Majesty, Gangrel = Protean, Mekhet = Auspex, Nosferatu = Obfuscate, Ventrue = Dominate
answers.q25: Independent study (hard, takes heaps of time and XP), mentorship from someone who _does_ have that Discipline, consuming the vitae of a Kindred with that Discipline
answers.q26: I think this is blood potency?
answers.q30: Crúac, Invictus Oaths, Theban, Carthian Laws
answers.q35: Being formally accepted into the covenant (maybe with Status - unsure about this)
answers.q39: Covenant powers are a reflection of a covenant's politics, ideology, and worldview (among other things).
answers.q40: It's a connection between two Kindred who have 'shared blood'. Most common example is that between a sire and their childe. Can range from an empathic link to an ability to directly communicate.
answers.q41: Bloodlines are sub-sets of clans. Looking at the rules you can only join a bloodline that resides within your clan. Side-note: Brujah have been demoted to being a subset of Gangrel?!
answers.q42: Daeva - the social predators; Nosferatu - the hidden monsters; Ventrue - the aristocratic lords; Gangrel - the outsider savages; Mekhet - the shadowy scholars

answers.q07: Sunlight, fire and going and getting your heart staked (don't do that)
answers.q10: Frenzy is what the Beast taking over completely is called. This can happen in reaction to immediate threats of danger (fire or low health, for example). However, threat of frenzy can increase over time due to consistent hunger, or continued social ostracisation that threatens the vampires ability to "live" in relative peace
answers.q11: The Masquerade, The Progeny, The Amaranth
answers.q12: Invictus, Carthian, Circle of the Crone, Lancea Et Sanctum
answers.q15: Unaligned vampires do not owe any loyalties to a covenant. These vampires gain no protections or benefits from other vampires on a covenant basis due to this, and find it hard to build trust with any vampires due to this lack of covenant (as a verb). Some Praxis holders may require kindred to join a covenant in order to remain welcome at court.
answers.q18: Vampires are social creatures. They rely on their connections with other kindred and systems they create together to survive and foster a vampire-safe environment. They are squishy compared to other non-human creatures and are stronger in numbers. Coming to court allows them to achieve this. Vampires also seek control, which they cannot easily find outside of the context of a court, through tenuous allyship with other vampires.
answers.q19: The territory that a vampire feeds from is called a Domain. This can often cover a city and is typically managed by that city’s court.
answers.q25: A kindred seeking to learn a discipline outside of their clan must find somebody from the relevant clan to teach them who already has a dot in that discipline. Not only must they convince them to do so, but they must risk forming a blood-bond with that vampire, as well as vitae addiction.
answers.q30: Oaths, Carthian Law, Cruac, Theban Sorcery
answers.q35: Vampires can learn covenant blood sorceries by way of mentorship from members of a covenant they share (Lance, Crone), or by way of deep study in order to achieve it without mentorship. They can also learn blood sorceries via diablery. Bad things happen to vampires who practice crucac outside of the Crone. Theban sorcery can only be used by Vampires with status one or higher in the Lance, and requires a Humanity rating equal to or higher than the dot rating  of the targeted Theban ritual
answers.q39: Disciplines are powers that improve on a vampires abilities as they are. Covenant powers have more external effects
answers.q40: The connection between sire and childe that allows them to feel when the other is in danger
answers.q41: Clans are the five basic types of vampires, bloodlines are specific ancestries of vampires?

answers.q07: Sunlight, Fire, and a wooden stake to the heart which brings on immediate Torpor.
answers.q08: The Beast, Blood Addiction, the necessity for Vitae to fuel their living death and supernatural abilities.
answers.q10: They enter what is called a Frenzy.
answers.q11: The Masquerade, Progeny, and Amaranth. Do not reveal yourself to mortals. Any Childe you Sire is your responsibility. Do not consume the soul of another Kindred.
answers.q12: The Invictus, The Carthian Movement, The Circle of the Crone, and the Lancea Et Sanctum.
answers.q13: Neonate - Less than 50 years, Ancillae - 50 to 150 years, and Elder - 150+
answers.q15: They will likely die or be enslaved by others who outnumber and outmatch them through combined resources if nothing else..
answers.q18: It is an opportunity to meet with their own kind, learn of the goings on in the city, make alliances, maneuver politically, deliver news and make appeals to the Prince, and an opportunity to let loose and be themselves for a bit.
answers.q20: Disciplines can be used, only physical harm is forbidden.
answers.q22: Daeva - Majesty, Gangrel - Protean, Mekhet - Auspex, Nosferatu - Nightmare, Ventrue - Dominate.
answers.q25: The disciplines of Obfuscate, Animalism, and the physical disciplines of Celerity, Resistance and Vigor can all be spontaneously developed by Kindred. The clan specific disciplines of Auspex, Dominate, Protean, Majesty and Nightmare can only be acquired outside their respective clans by imbibing some of the blood of a Kindred who possesses the power, and then undergoing training to learn to wield it.
answers.q30: Carthian Law, Invictus Oaths, Cruac, and Theban Sorcery
answers.q35: To learn Cruac rituals or Theban Sorcery, a Kindred must be a member in good standing of The Circle of the Crone, or the Lancea et Sanctum respectively. If a Kindred falls out of favour and loses all status among the Covenant, they can still perform the rituals, but they will not be able to learn any more.
answers.q39: They are usually tied to Covenant Status. You often require a certain level of Covenant Status to acquire these powers, and while you can have status in multiple covenants, you can only have a maximum of 5 covenant status dots in total, which will limit the level of power you can obtain if they are spread out.
answers.q40: Blood Sympathy is the connection between Kindred who are "Blood Relatives", whether Sire and Childe, Childer of the same Sire, even down to members of the same Clan. Kindred who have a bond of Blood Sympathy may sense when a sympathetic Kindred is put into Torpor or destroyed, and they can even communicate with one another directly by spending a willpower to force the activation of their Blood Sympathy. Blood Sympathy requires a successful roll of BP + Blood Sympathy, which ranges from +1 to +3 depending on how close the relation is.
answers.q41: A Clan is the overarching group of Kindred you come from - be that Daeva, Nosferatu, Ventrue, Gangrel or Mekhet. Bloodlines are specialized deviations within those Clans which may possess secondary abilities but still fall within the umbrella of the parent clan.
answers.q42: Daeva - the Serpents. Highly emotional and manipulative. Nosferatu - the Haunts. They hide in the shadows and broker information. Ventrue - the Lords. Always seeking power and control. Gangrel - the Savages. Bruisers and warriors, they are quick to anger and hard to kill. Mekhet - the Shadows. A little weird, but very good spies and assassins who often exhibit specialised abilities.

answers.q35: Don't actually have to be a member per se. It is enough to have status - which is a very subtle difference, and maybe not a difference depending on how we are defining "membership".

answers.q15: Unaligned face social, safety and political disadvantages
answers.q18: to gain status and influence, information
answers.q25: tasting Vitae of someone with the discipline
answers.q30: Oaths (Invictus), Cruac (Crone), Theban Sorcery (Lancea) & Carthian Law
answers.q35: membership of that covenant, status, a mentor and other costs
answers.q39: purchased like merits, but are cast as rituals
answers.q40: a shared connection by blood relatives who can sense each other
answers.q41: a bloodline is a sub-category of a clan
answers.q42: Daeva are socialites, Gengrel are warriors, Mekhet are occultists, Nosferatu are enforcers, Ventrue are leaders

answers.q07: Sun, Fire and Blood Dependancy. Vampires are also vulnerable to so-called "True Faith".
answers.q10: Frenzy, of which there are a few types: Rage (fury/insults), Hunger (Wassail/blood lust), and Fear (Rötschreck/fire or sunlight).
answers.q12: Lancea et Sanctum, Circle of the Crone, Carthians and Invictus
answers.q15: The Unaligned generally don’t last long
answers.q18: Influence, and coveted access to the Rack
answers.q20: Any sort of physical or mystical confrontation is strictly forbidden within Elysium.
answers.q22: Daeva - Majesty, Gangrel - Protean, Mekhet - Auspex, Nosferatu - Nightmare, Ventrue - Dominate
answers.q30: Cruac Sorcery, Theban Sorcery, Blood Oaths, Carthian Law
answers.q39: Multiple kindred can work together on rituals.
answers.q40: Blood sympathy is when a kindred can sense the strong emotions of another Kindred, and it’s nearly always when the kindred are “relatives”, or within two “steps” of the sire-childe connection from each other.
answers.q41: Bloodline is your sire’s specific line of sires. A clan is multiple bloodlines
answers.q42: Gangrel: wild barbarians. Nosferatu: hideous creeps, Venture; temporarily inconvenienced kings, Daeva; gorgeous and vapid socialites, Mekhet; shadowmancers and augers

answers.q02: The Beast. Sometimes ‘yo mama’ when being disrespectful to their rivals.
answers.q06: Touchstones. ‘Yo mama’ might be literal here.
answers.q07: The banes of sunlight, fire and staking.
answers.q08: Their hunger, their Beast, their predatory nature.
answers.q10: Frenzy. Usually severe violence. Lack of control. Panicked predator, bull in a china shop…there’s a yo mama joke but it’s feeling cringe now.
answers.q11: Masquerade - don’t reveal vampires to mortals. Progeny - don’t create new vampires carelessly, and they’re your responsibility. Amaranth - don’t be a soul-eating cannibal.
answers.q12: Carthian Movement, Invictus, Circle of the Crone, Lancea et Sanctum
answers.q15: They viewed with suspicion and pushed to join. Otherwise they’re likely to degenerate and become draugr, losing humanity and becoming risks to all vampires. Plus vampires are cliquey bitches and no-one likes a splitter.
answers.q18: They need the social connection to slow down the crazy, manage the beast and find purpose in immortality.
answers.q20: Yes. Might not be a good idea. Don’t get caught, or be scary enough to get caught and get away with it anyway.
answers.q22: Daeva: Majesty. Gangrel: Protean. Mekhet: Auspex. Nosferatu: Nightmare. Ventrue: Dominate.
answers.q25: By spontaneous manifestation / effort, unless it’s a Clan unique Discipline, in which cas they have to imbibe Vitae from a Kindred with that Discipline.
answers.q27: Vitae is most common; sometimes Willpower.
answers.q30: Carthian Law (eg Weaponise Dissent), Invictus Oaths (eg Oath of Action), Cruac (eg Cheval), Theban Sorcery (eg Blood Scourge).
answers.q35: Being a member of that covenant, or being taught by another kindred who is probably about to get murdered for sharing covenant secrets. Rarely a non-member can learn a little Cruac via the Chorister merit or (even more rarely) Theban Sorcery via the Laity merit.
answers.q36: Willpower; Theban also needs physical sacraments (sacrifices).
answers.q38: Yes, but teaching them is generally a death sentence and even using them is frowned upon at a minimum.
answers.q39: They MUST be taught and can’t be learned via the Blood. They often have more arcane or niche effects and can be less localised to the user.
answers.q40: Essentially allows related vampires to know what each other are up to. Generally triggered by extreme emotions and events. Automatically occurs on Final Death/torpor, sensed symbolically. General state can be sent to once/twice removed with 1 Willpower and Blood Potency roll. Detect/track with Wits + Blood Potency + Blood Sympathy.
answers.q41: A clan is a broad family representing a Vanpiric archetype. A bloodline is a narrow family lineage (though you can join despite a lack of lineage) which have more particular powers and weaknesses.
answers.q42: Daeva - Succubi, Anne Rice types, Twilight goes if you’re being harsh. Gangrel - savages, bestial shapeshifters, nature-attuned hippies, mystics. Mekhet - shadows; the umbral watchers, the unseen seers, peeping Toms, vicarious perverts. Nosferatu - Haunts; the ugly scary ones, smelly sewer dwellers, probably the most honest about what they really are. Ventrue - Lords; Dracula wannabes, aristocratic authoritarians, crazy inbred nobility.

answers.q04: The trait that determines how POTENT a vampires BLOOD is? Probably something silly like Blood Potency.
answers.q07: Weakness to Sunlight/Fire, Steak though the heart? Though I mean that also kills people too so I'd be hesitant to call it an exact weakness. I forgot to finish this answer before.
answers.q08: The beast and hunger that drives said beast, the desire for Vitae. Ironically if they don't hunt they get even more monstrous.
answers.q10: Frenzy- or alternatively a very long German word that I forget. Or, if it is a completle loss of control- they would become a Draugr, whom has forgotten their humanity in favor of being driven by the beast within.
answers.q11: 1. Masqurade- Don't get caught doing vampire shit by non-vampires 2. Progeny- make any children it's your problem, you take their mistakes 3. Amaranth don't diablerize (And the secret 4th tradition- Do all of these you like, just don't get caught.)
answers.q12: Carthians- Reformists, Invictus- Old money, Order of the Crone- Witches of the Blood, Lancea Sanctum- Religious Vampires, (Ordo Dracul- 'Sir. Not Appearing in this Film)
answers.q15: To be 'unaligned' or 'unbound' is a dangerous prospect without the backing of a group- either being very young and inexperienced, or very old and stable enough in their own power to be able to hold themselves up without additional support. Sometimes they are used as intermediaries between covenants- I'm sure there's more on this but honestly there's not as much on these as I thought there was in the V2 book, and from my looking around usually people who don't get help from covenants are taken advantage of real quick if they're not careful.
answers.q17: Harpy, Prince (Does that count?), Seneshal, Sherif, Hound,
answers.q18: Court is often held on an Elysium, which garantees a modicum of saftey- in addition to the fact that court is a place where people negotiate, make deals, organize, and update eachother on what's going on. If you want any sort of say or influence on the domain you find yourself in, or just an idea about what's going on- you need to go to court. Arguably it is more dangerous not to go to court, as it makes you blind to the machinations and goings on of other kindred, and the situation of Kindred in the city as a whole.
answers.q25: By either learning it themselves with the non-clan unique disciples, or getting a teacher and imbibing blood for the clan specific ones.
answers.q30: Carthian Law, Invictus Oaths, Cruac, Theban Sorcery
answers.q31: Carthian Law can stop people from using certain powers- but I'd also argue there's some blood sorceries that can do the same.
answers.q35: If you have status in the appropraite covenant you can learn new sorceries
answers.q36: Cruac- *MORE* Vitae, Theban- Willpower, plus a sacrifice and request for both. And for the NON-blood sorcery powers we have Carthian Law which is kind of fueled by legal precident? and Invictus Oaths wich is fueled by a promise or deal.
answers.q38: Yes- if you lose all your dots in Crone/Lancea status you can still cast/use the Sorceries you already know- however you are unable to learn new ones, and if you buy additional Cruac/Theban dots you don't get the regular free sorcery that comes with it. Many Invictus Oaths rely upon your dots in status in Invictus so switching out would render many of them moot- likewise with many aspects of Carthian law- but I assume it'd be the same where you couldn't learn new ones but keep the ones you have- perhaps if one is completely useless merit protection would come into effect and you would be refunded? Then again, if you're making this bed you're sleeping in it and probably already know the consiquences of leaving your covenant.
answers.q39: You can use them in different ways, and they all require some dots in their respective covenant to learn. They are also deeply tied to the philosophies and methods of their convenants- Cathians can weild legal precedent for themselves and against other covenants that might create a 'rules for thee, and not for me' situation- whilst Invictus have a tangible way of enforcing promises and traditions. The Lance and Crone, two sides of the same coin, are much more deeply connected to the more supernatural aspects of their natures and so have access to much more flexible (if costly) methods of manipulating that nature.
answers.q40: Blood sympathy is how close your blood is to someone else's- like family. It ranges from Once removed- your sire (That gives a +3 to all sympathy rolls), Twice Removed (+2, Siblings and Grandchilder/Grandsires), Thrive Removed (+1, Sire's siblings, great grandsire/childer), Four times removed (All clanmates, no modifiers)  Sympathy can notify you when one of your blood relations enters torpor, or sufferes final death- as well as a connection to their emotional state depending on the situation of the relitives. In addition there are powers that can use blood sympathy to increase their efficacy- Usually Cruac
answers.q41: Clans are a genus, Bloodlines are a species. Basically bloodlines are subcatagories of clans that have been made or manifested in powerful kindred (6+ Blood potency) that they have passed down to their childer. They come with drawbacks and benefits and some unique devotions sometimes.
answers.q42: Daeva- Divas, Venture- Ventre Capatalists, Nosferatu- Nasty lurkers, Mehket- Maniacs, Gangrel- I can't think of a word for 'Wild people' that starts with G but rest assured I'll edit this if I find one.
answers.q44: Daeva- though honestly with the errata they seem more just 'hungry' than anything. I guess I can see the reasoning but I am curious as to why it was modified. (Thanks for answering!)

answers.q08: The desires of the beast, the eternal thirst
answers.q10: Frenzy. The Beast picks a goal and does everything in its power to achieve it. For creatures so obsessed with maintaining control, losing it at the wrong time can cost them everything they've worked hard to build, whether than be maintaining touch with their humanity, or their place within Kindred society
answers.q11: The Masquerade, the Progency, the Amaranth
answers.q12: The Carthian Movement, Invictus, Lancea et Sanctum, the Circle of Crones
answers.q15: They are sometimes known as 'anarchs' or 'autarkis' or simply the Unaligned. These Vampires tend to find out that others join covenants for a reason. The nights are long and dangerous, and vampires without allies typically live short lives. More than that, the pursuit of a common goal gives a vampire purpose outside of the selfishness of the Beast; it distracts them from the endless cycle of hunting and daysleep, and slows their descent into true beashood. Those without a covenant to give them purpose often find themselves quickly devolving in the wake of the monstrous nature
answers.q18: It is a place to make yourself known, a place to garner and express your power. More than that, it is a place to ensure local vampire society does not descend into reckless infighting; the tangled web of politics those who attend the courts weave serve not only to dominate their opposition, but also protect vampire society as a whole by (hopefully) enforcing the upholding of the Traditions
answers.q20: So long as they do not cause physical harm, yes
answers.q22: Daeva: Majesty, Gangrel: Protean, Mekhet: Auspex, Nosferatu: Nightmare, Ventue: Dominate
answers.q25: A vampire may learn any of the general Disciplines (Animalis, Celerity, Obfuscate, Resiliance, Vigor) independantly through time and effort (4xp). For a Clan's specific  Discipline, one must track down a teacher who knows that Discipline and drinking their Vitae. Lastly, one can learn a Devotion through hard work and study under a teacher who knows it (provided they have the dots in the requisit Disciplines) or, on rare occasions, may spontaneously develop them. When learning a Devotion one need not drink their Teachers vitae like one does with a Clan specific Discipline
answers.q26: Blood Potency, the power of the blood confers a level or protection from the supernatural
answers.q27: Vitae, time (in the form of different Actions)
answers.q30: Cruac, Theban Sorcery, Carthian Law, Invictus Oaths
answers.q31: Carthians (I don't know how I missed this the first time)
answers.q35: You must have good standing (at least 1 dot) with either the Lancea or the Circle to learn them... In theory. In practice one need only find someone willing to teach them (though your teacher may not be around for long if they are found teaching you)
answers.q36: Willpower or Covenant Status. More than that, ones ability to manifest Covenant Powers is tied to ones ability to find support within their covenant; Cruac is less taxing when the lost vitae is shared, many Carthian Laws only function via concensus, and Invictus may only benefit from an oath if they can find a participant willing to bind themselves to them (and a Notary willing to sanctify the Oath), and Theban Sorcerers may have an easier time summoning their Will amongst their fellow supplicants (or may have their supplicants provide a rare sacrament)
answers.q39: They typically require standing within a Covenant to access, and have additional restrictions on them depending on what Covenants power is being expressed. They represent the secrets guarded by their keepers, and coveted by outsiders. Where regular Disciplines are manifestation of the Beasts power, Covenant Powers are an expression of the power vampires have built for themselves, a power that sometimes finds its roots in the Beast, but transforms it into something new, distinct, and powerful
answers.q40: Blood calls to blood. You feel a level of innate, supernatural connection to all those you share a heritage with (whether you are they are a direct descendant, or simply share a clan)
answers.q41: A bloodline can be thought of as offspring of a Clan; Each bloodline can be traced back to a an individual who belonged to a clan, but the bloodline is unmistakably something different from its source, carrying its own benefits and banes
answers.q42: Daeva: The dazzling socialite. Gangrel: The roughneck beasts. Mekhet: The academics and occultists. Nosferatu: Monsters in the shadows. Ventrue: The leader and dominators

answers.q12: Lancea Sanctum, Carthians, Circle of the Crone, Invictus
answers.q22: Daeva: Majesty, Gangrel: Protean, Mekhtet: Auspex, Nosferatu: Nightmare, Ventrue: Dominate
answers.q30: Cruac, Theban, Carthian Law, Invictus Oaths
answers.q35: They have to be an acknowledged member of that particular covenant
answers.q39: A vampire from any clan can learn them as long as they are a part of that covenant.
answers.q40: A connection between "related" vampires that can happen when they are nearby or when experiencing strong emotions or sensations
answers.q41: Every vampire is part of a clan (like a "species") while bloodlines are a smaller "family" that branches off from a clan.
answers.q42: Gangrel - Fighter, Nosferatu - Outsider, Mekhet - Spy, Ventrue - Leader, Daeva - Seducer

answers.q10: the beast takes over and sends the character into an unthinking frenzy. depending on what triggered the frenzy (fear, hunger, fight) the beast is now making the choices unless the character sucessfully RIDES THE WAVE. A touchstone can also make a series of checks to talk a vampire down from the frenzy. Doing anything against the beast requires willpower and a RESOLVE+COMPOSURE resistance roll
answers.q12: Crone, Invictus, Carthian, Lancea Et Sanctum
answers.q15: Unbound. this is not a good place to be in a vampire society of status and intrigue. No friends of consequence, less access to resources like feeding grounds. Cant get covenant benefits. It becomes possible for powerful Vampires to exploit you without political consequences. VAMPS TOGETHER STRONG...
answers.q18: The dangers of not attending court are worse. The court is the central playground for schemes of influences, corruption, politic and manipulation to play out. My Nos tries to sit in the hidden center of a web of influence, partonage, implied threats. Notice how the spider sits still, unmoving right in the middle so that it can feel everyvibration through its network directly. that is why I come to court. The schemes grow - only to have my agents overhear rival plans as they form. In court I can trade favours to act against my enemies. I can build alliance, learn more, observe undetected. Yes, court is dangerous. The upsides of court, that ability to acumulate and shift alliances and power structures through manipulation or direct violence, are what makes it NECESSARY for vampires of a certain motivation..
answers.q20: yes, the are regional customs against feeding but nothing in the rules prohibits Disciplines or any dread power
answers.q22: D: Majesty, G:Protean, M:Auspex, N:Nightmare, V:Dominate
answers.q25: Common Disciplines (the standard ten that appear across clans): Require tasting the Vitae of someone who has it + a teacher + extra XP. Covenant-specific powers (Cruac, Theban Sorcery, Coils of the Dragon): Require covenant membership/status and specific instruction within that tradition. No Vitae-tasting needed, but you must belong to the covenant. Bloodline Disciplines: Require joining a specific bloodline to access their unique Discipline.
answers.q30: Cruac sorcery, Theban Sorcery, Carthian Law, Oaths
answers.q35: be a member of the covenant basically. no outsiders! Cruac requires being an acolyte in good standing. Theban requires a priest of L et S. You can keep your abiliities if you lose your standing but youre now a target and you cant learn new disciplines
answers.q37: Cruac Sorcery taps into the RAW beast
answers.q38: yeah. you cant unlearn but wont progress
answers.q39: Covenant powers come from technique, learning and collective willnot blood
answers.q40: inante connection between bloodline relatives.  Strength depends on the lineage distance between individuals. This can be a message sent under duress with a chance to be detected by "nearby" kin DIRECT (awareness, connection, sympathy). +3 mod on sympathy rolls. no limit to range  CLOSE (sibling, connection, slight empathy), +2 on sympathy rolls, on the same continent.  REMOVED (faint impressions)  +1 on sympathy rolls, within the same city  DISTANT (barely anything except a hint) NO MODIFIERS, within a mile
answers.q41: You're Embraced into a clan, but you choose to join or found a bloodline. you can be inducted into a bloodline you weren't sired into — by drinking the blood of an existing member (an Avus) and undergoing the joining process. So it's more like a mystical guild than a strict family tree. bloodlines typically grant access to a unique fourth Discipline exclusive to that bloodline, but also impose an additional bane on top of your clan bane
